summary: Maternal anti-B antibodies crossing the placenta lead to hemolysis of fetal red blood cells in ABO-incompatible neonates, resulting in unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia characteristic of mild to moderate jaundice. This pathway occurs spontaneously during first pregnancies unlike Rh incompatibility, which requires previous sensitization and manifests differently clinically with worse laboratory findings such as markedly increased bilirubin and significant anemia.
